included_relationships.notes: Covered relationships are spouse, son, daughter, or parent of the employee who is a covered military member on covered active duty or called to covered active duty.

required_value: notified of impending call or order to covered active duty

required_value.field: related to active duty or call to active duty

required_value: school or daycare enrollment or transfer

required_value.required_value: make or update financial or legal arrangements
short-term temporary rest and recuperation leave during deployment

required_value.field: days after termination of active duty status

required_value.required_value: parent of covered military member incapable of self-care

required_value: admitting or transferring to care facility

conditions.notes: Covered active duty means deployment with the Armed Forces to a foreign country, or under a Federal call or order to active duty in support of a contingency operation.
conditions.source_text: An eligible employee may take FMLA leave while the employee's spouse, son, daughter, or parent is on covered active duty or call to covered active duty status... Eligible employees may take qualifying exigency leave for any of the following reasons...

relationship_issues: included_relationships.required_value uses 'child' but the regulation text specifies 'son or daughter'; should be corrected to match the regulation wording
logic_issues: short-notice deployment nested check includes 'active duty support == contingency operation' as a separate sub-check, but the regulation ties contingency operation to the covered active duty definition, not as an additional exigency-type requirement; the short-notice deployment exigency is simply notice of 7 or fewer calendar days before deployment

rest_and_recuperation nested check uses field 'during deployment' with required_value 'short-term temporary rest and recuperation leave', which conflates the field label and value; field should describe what is being checked and required_value should be the qualifying value

post-deployment activities uses 'days after duty ends' but the regulation says '90 days following the termination of the covered military member's active duty status'; unit should reflect this precisely
format_issues: nested check objects inside qualifying_exigency.required_value (the ANY_OF list) include extra keys 'citation' and 'unit' at the same level as field/operator/required_value/unit; nested check objects must use exactly field, operator, required_value, unit — no citation key is permitted inside nested checks

the outer qualifying_exigency condition uses operator ALL_OF but required_value is a list of two items where the second is itself an ANY_OF nested check; this is valid structurally but the inner ANY_OF check object inside required_value carries extra keys (citation, unit at wrong level)
revision_summary: Corrected included_relationships.required_value from 'child' to 'son or daughter'
Removed extra 'citation' keys from nested check objects inside required_value lists
Simplified short-notice deployment sub-check to remove unsupported 'contingency operation' sub-requirement from the exigency type level
Corrected rest_and_recuperation field/required_value to be clear and atomic
Corrected post-deployment 'days after duty ends' unit to 'days after termination of active duty status'
Preserved all other API1 content which is well-grounded in the regulation chunks
